evaluations.name: Business Hours Energy Profile
evaluations.message: Office follows clean 9-to-5 pattern — 37% of energy in business hours.
evaluations.recommendation: Baseline established — deviations indicate schedule issues.

evaluations.name: Monday Morning Ramp-Up
evaluations.recommendation: Pre-cool Friday evening to reduce Monday morning HVAC surge.

evaluations.name: High Night-Time Base Load
evaluations.message: Building is consuming 0.795% of daytime energy overnight.
evaluations.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.

evaluations.name: Weekend Consumption Anomaly
evaluations.message: Weekend usage is 0.859% of weekday — expected <30%.
evaluations.recommendation: Review weekend HVAC schedules and unoccupied equipment.

evaluations.name: Demand Spike Detection
evaluations.message: Peak demand is 1.9x the average — opportunity to flatten load curve.
evaluations.recommendation: Implement demand response: stagger HVAC start, shift non-critical loads.

evaluations.name: Overnight Equipment Load
evaluations.recommendation: Audit servers, HVAC, and lighting auto-shutoff schedules.

evaluations.name: Weather Sensitivity Check
evaluations.recommendation: Optimize HVAC based on temperature forecast to reduce reactive energy spikes.

evaluations.name: Seasonal Energy Pattern
evaluations.recommendation: Seasonal HVAC tuning can yield 10-20% energy savings.

evaluations.name: High Consumption During Low-Occupancy Hours
evaluations.recommendation: Configure occupancy sensors and timer-based shutdowns.

evaluations.name: Disproportionate Cooling Load
evaluations.recommendation: Review thermostat setpoints — natural ventilation viable below 22°C.

alerts.name: High Night-Time Base Load
alerts.message: Building is consuming 0.795% of daytime energy overnight.
alerts.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.

alerts.name: Weekend Consumption Anomaly

alerts.recommendation: Review weekend HVAC schedules and unoccupied equipment.
